title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Mr. Satterthwaite and Dr. Horton discuss the motives behind the attempted poisoning, concluding that the woman's actions were driven by greed. Satterthwaite explains that she sought to secure a fortune for her son, Roland, by targeting the inheritance of Tom Edison’s grandson. After Dr. Horton departs, Satterthwaite experiences a vision of a tall, slender woman named Lily wearing a pearl-colored dress. He assures her that her son is safe; she smiles, kisses her hand, and walks toward a burning straw man in a nearby field. Satterthwaite reflects that she belongs to a world of love and death. Following this, a small black dog named Hermes approaches Satterthwaite with a note from "H.Q." congratulating him.

In a different setting, fifty-eight-year-old Mr. Isaac Bonts arrives at Dartmouth on his yacht, "The Mermaid," accompanied by Leo Stein, Sir George, Madame Marlowe, Samuel Lathen, Eve Lathen, Lady Lathen, and Evan Llewellyn. After watching a rowing race, the group prepares to visit fairgrounds for amusement. While Eve Lathen expresses a desire to visit a gypsy caravan, Mr. Bournes suggests dinner at the George Hotel. During the gathering, Leo Stein and Mr. Böhmermann play a game of darts. Observations are made regarding Evan Llewellyn’s distracted behavior and the alertness of Sir George and Madame Marlowe. The narrative introduces Miss Jennet Lestrange, a charming but distant woman, and a golden-haired girl named Yvonne, who approaches her and Ewan Llewellyn.
